name: BBQ Chicken and Andouille Hash with Poached E
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Cooked potatoes; diced into 1/2 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------POACHED EGGS-----------------------
4 Eggs
3 tbsp Green onions; sliced
Sauce Picante (recipe separate)
Heat the grill or broiler. Season the chicken with salt and pepper. Brush the BBQ Sauce on the chicken, coating the breast completely. Place the chicken on the hot grill or broiler and cook for 5-6 minutes on each side. Set aside and cool.
For hash: In a saute pan heat the oil. Add the potatoes and saute, shaking the pan occasionally, until lightly browned, about 2 minutes. Add the onions, shallots, and Andouille sausage and stir fry for 1 minute. Dice the BBQ chicken and add to the Andouille mixture and saute 1 minute. Add the garlic and season with salt and pepper, stirring occasionally for 4 minutes.
For poached eggs: Bring 3 cups of water to a boil with 1/2 teaspoon white vinegar and 1/2 teaspoon salt in a small saucepan over high heat. Crack an egg into a cup and slide the egg gently into the water. Crack another egg into the cup and, when the water returns to a boil, reduce the heat to low and simmer until the eggs are set, about 2-2 1/2 minutes. Drain on paper towels.
To assemble: Spoon a small amount of picante sauce in the center of the plate. Place 3/4 cup of the hash on the sauce. Top with poached egg and garnish with shaved green onions.
